80802.title: Halifax Pride Parade
80802.description: PIPSC members are invited to march with us in the Halifax Pride Parade in support of the 2SLGBTQIA+ community. This year, PIPSC will be joining the Nova Scotia Federation of Labour (NSFL) and will be marching alongside fellow union members from organizations such as CAPE, PSAC, and others. Family members and friends are welcome to participate as well. We hope you’ll join us for this celebration of diversity, inclusion, and solidarity! We are also pleased to be joined by Katie Francis, one of PIPSC’s Full-Time Vice-Presidents and a member of the Institute’s Board of Directors. Katie Francis is currently in her three-year term, helping to guide the strategic direction of PIPSC and advance key initiatives on behalf of members. Her portfolios include Return-to-Office (RTO), Phoenix, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion (EDI). Looking forward to seeing you there!Parade Date: Saturday, July 18, 2026 Location: Parade Mustering Grounds – 5775 Cogswell St., Halifax, NS. Our parade spot is C24, and you will find us in the parade mustering grounds, which are located along Cogswell Street, between Robie Street and North Park Street. Time: We will start gathering at 11:00 am. The parade starts at noon, but it’s important that volunteers are in place ahead of that time. Additional Info: Make sure to dress for the weather, pack a reusable water bottle, plenty of sunscreen and a hat. To register, please fill out this form by end of day, July 17, 2026. If you have questions, please contact Olg

80842.title: Return to office – Complimentary mice and BYO-Desk
80842.description: Join us at the corner of Bank Street and Laurier Avenue in Ottawa, as we spread the word about workplace health and safety concerns you’ve shared with us. When: July 8 from 12:00 to 1:00 PM ET Where: Corner of Laurier Avenue and Bank Street, Ottawa RSVP NOW We’ll have treats and information about your rights regarding RTO. You’ll also learn how to participate in our RTO sticker contest – stick it, post a photo, tag PIPSC, and you could win a prize! We’ve heard hundreds of stories about: offices that feel like construction sites employees being forced to work from cafeterias and crowded cubicles outdated government offices with pest problems, and more Let’s continue to push back against this one-size-fits-all approach, which lacks evidence to support it, puts workers’ health and safety at risk, and undermines employee productivity. If you have any questions, please email Kfrancis@pipsc.ca.

81115.title: Occupational Health and Safety Training – October 23 & 24, 2026
81115.description: We are pleased to offer an Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) training in addition to the employer training you may have received. As we are limited to twenty participants, we will use selection criteria to select the participants. We encourage you to submit your application form as soon as you receive the email. Participants will learn: What health and safety entails. An overview of occupational health and safety codes which applies to the federal, provincial and territorial sectors. The roles and responsibilities of employers under various codes. The roles and responsibilities of bargaining agents under various codes; and Their role as a steward and PIPSC activist in promoting a safe and healthy workplace. LOCATION: Calgary, Alta TIME: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (All participants are required to attend the full session) APPLICATION DEADLINE: August 14, 2026 APPLICATION FORM: https://forms.gle/mzictDaLCpAE46nv6 After the application deadline, the training committee will review your application, if selected to attend we will send you a registration invite. Once you complete your registration, then you will get a confirmation email with the meeting ID Code and other information about the OHS training. Please do not book any travel arrangements or hotel reservations until you have received confirmation of attendance by e-mail from the Prairie/NWT Regional Office. 81393.description: DATE: Saturday, August 29, 2026 TIME: 5:00 p.m. LOCATION: Mosaic Stadium, 1700 Elphinstone Street, Regina, SK PRICE: $20 for adults, $15 for children (ages 3 – 15) The PIPSC Regina Branch Executive is pleased to announce a Family Day Saskatchewan Roughrider Game Event for PIPSC members and their families. We invite you to join us for an evening of football, family fun, and fellowship! This Week 13 matchup is the Family Day and Greatest Show on Turf carnival-themed game, featuring post-game field access and the Saskatchewan Roughriders’ Rider Nation Alternate uniforms. As an added benefit, members and their families will have access to exclusive pre-game end zone passes, providing a unique opportunity to get closer to the action before kickoff. Admission for PIPSC members and their families is $20 per adult and $15 per child (ages 3-15). The Regina Branch will subsidize the remaining cost of each ticket. Please note: Outside food and beverages are not permitted in the stadium. Registration Registration deadline: July 27, 2026 To register, please email lscott@pipsc.ca with the number of tickets you would like to request, indicating how many are adult and child tickets. location: Coronation park, Site 2, 11425-142 Street, NW, Edmonton
81602.description: The Edmonton Branch Executive is delighted to invite you and your family to our annual PIPSC Family Picnic. RAIN or SHINE, the picnic WILL NOT be cancelled. Date: Saturday August 8, 2026Time:11:00 am to 2:00 pmLocation: Coronation park, Site 2, 11425-142 Street, NW, Edmonton CoronationPark_SiteMap.jpg (800×618) Games for the Children! Food Served – 11:30 am. to 1:30 pm(Hot Dogs, Burgers, including Veggie Burgers, Sides & Beverages) RSVP by email to Jonathan Fedorow-Scott at jfedorowscott@pipsc.ca by August 1, 2026 with the following information: Full name Department Subgroup Number of Adults and Number of Children (including gender and ages) attending Please respond early as space is limited. *We are using members’ funds for this event; therefore, attendance will be limited to the members, members’ partners and their children living in the same household. Note: You can bring your own lawn chairs if you like as seating is limited. No reminder will be sent out. Please help us manage our funds and honor the registration you make. If your plans change, please be sure to advise us in advance so we can cancel your registration and another member may attend. Members who neither attend nor inform us that they will not be able to attend, may have their eligibility to attend future events affected.

location: Fresh Water Institute (DFO) Winnipeg Large Seminar Rm
81632.description: You’re invited to the SP Subgroup Collective Agreement Topics lunch & learn and Notice of AGM Date: August 20, 2026 Time: 1200 CT Location: Fresh Water Institute (DFO) Winnipeg Large Seminar Rm RSVP Deadline: August 15, 2026 HOW TO RSVP: 501 University Crescent Lunch and Learn 2026 MEETING OR EVENT INFORMATION – Lunch and information open to all PIPSC members working at the Fresh Water Institute AGENDA/ELECTION INFORMATION – Information will be provided on Overtime, Leave types and when to use them, Accommodation, SP Bargaining updates and Workforce Adjustment 501 University Crescent SP Subgroup AGM to occur following the presentations on Overtime, Leave and workforce adjustment. Voting limited to members in SP Group classifications. Elections will be held for all positions, as follows: · President – 2 year term · Vice President – 1 year term · Treasurer – 1 year term · Secretary – 2 year term · Members at Large (4)- one year term Nominations are accepted by email prior to the meeting or on the floor please reach out to an executive member listed below if you would like to run but can’t make meeting Tracey Loewen: Tracy.Loewen@dfo-mpo.gc.ca Sheila Atchison: Sheila.Atchison@dfo-mpo.gc.ca Regina Sobowale: Regina.Sobowale@dfo-mpo.gc.ca Jody Willis: Jody.Willis@dfo-mpo.gc.ca Tara Bortoluzzi: Tara.Bortoluzzi@dfo-mpo.gc.ca Brendan Spearin: Brendan.Spearin@dfo-mpo.gc.ca We look forward to seeing you there!
